note: Must score at least 55% in Class 7 (relaxable by 5% for SC/ST). Only for govt/govt-aided/local body schools, not KV, Navodaya, or private schools. Must maintain 60% in Class 10 and clear promotion each year to continue.
description: Monthly merit scholarship for Class 9–12 students from economically weaker government/government-aided schools.
benefits: ₹12,000/year (₹1,000/month) stipend

note: Maximum 2 girls per family. Admission must be through centralised State/Central counselling at an AICTE-approved institute, not management quota. Apply via National Scholarship Portal.
description: Financial assistance for female students pursuing AICTE-approved technical Diploma/Degree courses.
benefits: ₹50,000/year for up to 3 years

note: Requires a minimum 40% disability certified by a government medical board (private certificates not accepted). Management quota admissions are not eligible.
description: Financial support for specially-abled students pursuing AICTE-approved technical education.

note: Selection method has shifted toward merit on qualifying-class marks (the older YASASVI Entrance Test was cancelled for recent cycles) — confirm current method on NSP before assuming.
description: Top-class school scholarship for OBC/EBC/DNT students in Class 9–12.
benefits: ₹1.25 lakh/year (Class 11–12)

note: Must be in the top 20th percentile of your board's Class 12 results. Not available for diploma, distance, or correspondence courses. 50% of scholarships reserved for girls.
description: Merit scholarship for top-performing Class 12 students pursuing undergraduate/postgraduate courses.
benefits: ₹10,000–20,000/year depending on course level

note: Must be renewed every academic year. Apply via NSP or your state's SC scholarship portal. Aadhaar-linked bank account required for DBT.
description: Post-matric financial assistance for SC students from Class 11 through Ph.D.
benefits: Fee reimbursement plus maintenance allowance

note: Apply separately at maef.nic.in, not always bundled with main NSP flow. Benefit amount is unverified — sources disagree, confirm on official site before finalizing.
description: Merit scholarship for minority girl students studying in Class 9–12.
benefits: ~₹6,000/year (Class 11–12) — verify on official site

note: Certificate must explicitly say 'Non-Creamy Layer', not just OBC. Income ceiling appears revised upward to 2.5 lakh in recent cycles — older sources cite 1.5 lakh, verify current figure.
description: Post-matric financial assistance for Non-Creamy Layer OBC students.
benefits: Maintenance allowance plus fee reimbursement (varies by course)

note: No income criteria — purely merit/rank based. NOT for Engineering, Medicine, or other professional courses. Eligible via top 1% in Class 12 boards, top 10,000 JEE/NEET rank, IISER/NISER admission, or KVPY/NTSE/JBNSTS/Olympiad medal.
description: Merit-based scholarship for science students based on board rank, entrance exam rank, or national exam medals.
benefits: ₹80,000/year for up to 5 years

note: Only for students who are BOTH domiciled in and studying within West Bengal. Minimum marks requirement varies by level (60% commonly cited for HS/UG). Cannot be combined with Kanyashree K3 for the same course.
description: Merit-cum-means scholarship for students domiciled in and studying within West Bengal.
benefits: ₹1,000–8,000/month depending on course level

note: Implemented by the Ministry of Tribal Affairs (separate from SC/OBC schemes run by the Social Justice ministry). Funds shared 75:25 between Centre and State (90:10 for NE/special category states).
description: Post-matric financial assistance for ST students from Class 11 through Ph.D.
benefits: Fee reimbursement plus ₹225–₹525/month maintenance allowance

note: Scope appears narrowed in recent cycles from Class 1–10 down to mainly Class 9–10 — verify current class range on NSP. Max 2 children per family eligible; 30% of slots reserved for girls.
description: Pre-matric scholarship for minority students in Class 9–10.

scheme_name: Ayushman Bharat – Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PM-JAY)
note: Eligibility based on SECC 2011 data; operational across all 28 states and 8 UTs as of June 2026, with West Bengal joining June 8, 2026. No fixed income cap for SECC-listed categories — some state expansions also cover families up to ₹5 lakh income.
description: Free health insurance covering secondary and tertiary hospitalization for SECC-listed and state-expanded families.
benefits: Up to ₹5 lakh per family per year, cashless treatment at empanelled hospitals
Pre-existing diseases covered from day one
Senior citizens 70+ get an additional ₹5 lakh top-up via Ayushman Vay Vandana Card

note: Beneficiaries contribute ₹250–₹1000/month based on income; dependents include spouse, dependent parents, unmarried children up to 25. Official domain changed from cghs.gov.in to cghs.mohfw.gov.in in April 2025.
description: Healthcare scheme for Central Government employees and pensioners, with contribution scaled by pay/pension.
benefits: OPD, inpatient and AYUSH treatment coverage
Cashless treatment at empanelled hospitals
HDFC ERGO coverage up to ₹5 lakh per family per year

note: Applies to establishments with 10+ employees (20+ in Maharashtra/Chandigarh). Wage ceiling unchanged since January 2017. Ceiling is ₹25,000/month for persons with disabilities.
description: Medical and cash benefit scheme for workers earning up to ₹21,000/month at covered establishments.
benefits: Full medical care from day one of employment, no expenditure ceiling
Sickness benefit at 70% of wages for up to 91 days/year
Maternity benefit for 26 weeks at full wage

note: Also covers SC/ST, BPL, PM-JAY beneficiaries, and e-Shram/MGNREGA cardholders regardless of income. Excludes women in regular Central/State Govt or PSU employment or receiving similar statutory maternity benefits. Stackable with JSY.
description: Cash maternity benefit for women in their first, or second-if-girl-child, pregnancy.
benefits: ₹5,000 for first living child in two instalments
₹6,000 for second child if a girl, paid in one instalment after birth

note: BPL certification required in High Performing States (HPS); simplified criteria apply where BPL cards are unavailable. Covers up to two live births; women under 19 cannot register.
description: Cash incentive scheme promoting institutional delivery, especially for BPL women in low-resource states.
benefits: ₹1,400 for rural institutional delivery
₹500 for BPL home delivery
Amounts vary by state and LPS/HPS classification

scheme_name: Rashtriya Arogya Nidhi (RAN) incl. Health Minister's Cancer Patient Fund
note: Patient must belong to a BPL family; funds are paid directly to the treating government hospital, not the patient. Treatment only at government hospitals.
description: Financial assistance fund for BPL patients needing treatment for life-threatening or rare diseases at government hospitals.
benefits: Up to ₹15 lakh for life-threatening diseases
Up to ₹15 lakh under HMCPF for cancer treatment
Up to ₹50 lakh for rare diseases

note: Not an insurance scheme but a price-subsidy access program. Sanitary napkins available at ₹1/pad.
description: Price-subsidy program providing access to affordable generic medicines through Jan Aushadhi Kendras.
benefits: Generic medicines 50–90% cheaper than branded equivalents
14,000+ Jan Aushadhi Kendras nationwide
2,047+ medicines and 300+ surgical items available

scheme_name: PM RAHAT (Road Accident Victim Hospitalization and Assured Treatment)
note: Launched 13 Feb 2026; covers any road accident victim including hit-and-run cases. Delivered via PM-JAY empanelled hospitals, integrated with ERSS-112.
description: Cashless emergency trauma care for road accident victims, available regardless of income.
benefits: Cashless emergency treatment up to ₹1.5 lakh per victim
Covers first 7 days from date of accident
Stabilization guaranteed up to 24–48 hrs depending on severity

scheme_name: Chief Minister's Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme (CMCHIS)
note: Income threshold varies by source (₹72,000–₹1,20,000/year) — figure has likely been revised over time, verify current threshold before relying on it. Sri Lankan refugees, interstate migrants (6+ months residence), and rescued/orphaned girl children also eligible.
description: State health insurance scheme for Tamil Nadu families, also covering Sri Lankan refugees and long-term migrants.
benefits: Up to ₹5,00,000 per family per year on a floater basis
Covers major surgeries, critical care, diagnostics, and follow-ups

note: For families not covered under Ayushman Bharat; must not own more than 35 acres agricultural land; urban household must be under 3,000 sq.ft. BPL/white ration card holders also eligible.
description: State health insurance for Himachal Pradesh families not covered under Ayushman Bharat.
benefits: ₹5 lakh coverage per family
Cashless hospitalisation at government and empanelled private hospitals, in-state and select out-of-state

scheme_name: Sarbat Sehat Bima Yojana / Mukh Mantri Sehat Yojna (MMSY)
note: Originally limited to below ₹5 lakh income / BPL households, but expanded to all bonafide Punjab households irrespective of income after January 2026 integration into MMSY.
description: Punjab's universal state health insurance scheme, expanded beyond income-based eligibility since January 2026.
benefits: ₹10 lakh coverage per family
Covers 2,000 treatment packages
Pre-existing disease coverage from Day 1, no waiting period

scheme_name: Ayushman Bharat Health and Wellness Centres / Ayushman Arogya Mandir
note: Formerly 'Health & Wellness Centres,' rebranded 'Ayushman Arogya Mandir'; the primary-care pillar complementing the PM-JAY hospitalization pillar.
description: Free primary and preventive healthcare centres, upgrading Sub Health Centres and PHCs nationwide.
benefits: Free basic checkups and screenings
Preventive and primary care closer to the community
Target of 1,50,000 centres nationally
